name: Alice Coltrane
abstract: Alice Lucille Coltrane (née McLeod; August 27, 1937 – January 12, 2007), also known as Swamini Turiyasangitananda (IAST: Svāminī Turīyasaṅgītānanda) or simply Turiya, was an American jazz musician, composer, bandleader, and Hindu spiritual leader. An accomplished pianist and one of the few harpists in the history of jazz, Coltrane record…

about_quotes.speaker: Mcbee
about_quotes.evidence: I don’t know if you can compare your experience to someone else, but you played from ’69 to ’72 if my facts are correct, both with Alice Coltrane and Pharoah Sanders simultaneously? ... Mostly with Pharoah. I was in Pharoah’s band at the time. Occasionally I’d play a concert with Alice.

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: I did something called “The Reverend King Suite” which was actually written by John Coltrane, on our album called Cosmic Music with Alice and Rashied, Pharaoh Sanders.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Mcbee
about_quotes.evidence: when I played eventually played with Alice Coltrane... Alice Coltrane showed me that bass line. 'This is what I want you to play.'

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah, that was done up there at Fantasy Records. That was one of the times I was kind of dating her.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Branford Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: And it was…so they made a special CD and it was just us and Alice Coltrane on this one CD.
about_quotes.doc_title: Branford Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Ousley
about_quotes.evidence: And at that time Alice Coltrane, who was not Coltrane then, she was married to a singer, Poncho [Kenny] Hagood... And she was, they were living in Paris. She was a piano player there
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Ousley interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001
